input.background: Recall the illustrative example concerning a matched case-control study of adenomatous colon polyps and diet.
input.question: Test the association for significance. Show all hypothesis-testing steps.

output.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0$ : no association between fruit and vegetable consumption and colon polyps in the population versus $H_2: H_0$ false. B. Test statistic. $z_{\mathrm{McN}}=\operatorname{sqrt}\left[(45-24)^2 /(45+24)\right]=2.528$ C. $P$-value. $P=0.011$ (significant evidence) D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against $H_0$ is significant at $\alpha=0.05$ and is almost significant at $\alpha=0.01$.

case_control_status.description: Indicates whether the subject is a case (with adenomatous colon polyps) or a control (without adenomatous colon polyps).

dietary_factor.value: Various dietary factors (e.g., fiber intake, fat intake)
dietary_factor.description: Represents the dietary factors being studied for their association with adenomatous colon polyps.

matching_variable.value: Variables used for matching cases and controls (e.g., age, sex)
matching_variable.description: Variables used to match cases and controls to reduce confounding.
